channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Meta Oversight Board seriously concerned about Facebook’s ‘overenforcement’ of Harris-Walz nipple post
item.description: Meta Platforms’ Oversight Board on Wednesday told the company to keep up a Facebook post superimposing the faces of U.S.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: The best Python libraries for parallel processing
item.description: Python is powerful, versatile, and programmer-friendly, but it isn’t the fastest programming language around. Some of Python’s speed limitations are due to its default implementation, CPython, being single-threaded. That is, CPython doesn’t use more than one hardware thread at a time.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Facebook owner Meta is bringing back facial recognition tech. Here’s why
item.description: Three years after Meta shut down facial recognition software on Facebook amid a groundswell of privacy and regulator pushback, the social media giant said on Tuesday it is testing the service again as part of a crackdown on “celeb bait” scams.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Big offshore wind project proposed for New York as other sites are evaluated in 3 states
item.description: Offshore wind energy projects in New York, New Jersey and Maryland are moving forward, as federal regulators examine the proposals and opponents increase their legal challenges to the work.
item.dc:creator: Wayne Parry, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: October is Cybersecurity Awareness Month. Here’s how to stay safe from scams
item.description: As scammers find new ways to steal money and personal information, consumers should be more vigilant about who they trust, especially online.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: AI dominates Gartner’s 2025 predictions
item.description: Artificial Intelligence continues to dominate this week’s Gartner IT Symposium/Xpo, as well as the research firm’s annual predictions list.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The iPad mini is still the best small tablet around
item.description: We waited three years for Apple to update the iPad mini, and what we got is more of the same — boosted with a much better processor. Apple’s iPad mini has consistently been positioned as either the most versatile, or the most underpowered tablet in the company’s lineup.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Giant fish thought to be extinct is spotted in the Mekong River
item.description: Scientists say a giant fish in the Mekong River thought to be extinct has been spotted three times in recent years.
item.dc:creator: Christina Larson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Tech giants press Congress to codify AI Safety Institute
item.description: A coalition of more than 60 tech companies and industry groups, including Amazon, Anthropic, Google, Meta, Microsoft and OpenAI, urged congressional leaders Tuesday to pass legislation permanently authorizing the U.S. Artificial Intelligence Safety Institute by the end of the year.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Nvidia Partners with Vertiv to Revolutionize Data Center Cooling
item.description: Nvidia teams up with Vertiv to provide liquid cooling designs for AI factories, enabling efficient and scalable data centers.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Rust 1.82 brings Cargo info subcommand
item.description: Rust 1.82, the newest version of the popular memory-safe programming language, fulfills a longstanding request for displaying information in the Cargo package manager registry. Also with Rust 1.82, Apple macOS on 64-bit Arm now offers Rust’s highest guarantee of functionality.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Biden administration to provide $325 million for new Michigan semiconductor factory
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration said Monday that it would provide up to $325 million to Hemlock Semiconductor for a new factory, a move that could help give Democrats a political edge in the swing state of Michigan ahead of election day.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Top considerations for narrowing the federal cybersecurity skills gap
item.description: Addressing the federal cybersecurity skills gap requires a multifaceted approach in coordination with the private sector. The post Top considerations for narrowing the federal cybersecurity skills gap first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: GoFundMe bets social media can unlock Gen Z giving. Meta partnership, new tools will test that
item.description: “We play a really important role helping people ask for help and give help in the world,” GoFundMe CEO Tim Cadogan said.
item.dc:creator: JAMES POLLARD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Army aims for speed in new $10B software contract
item.description: Potential bidders have until the end of this week to offer comments on the Army's latest draft request for proposals. 15-20 firms will win spots on the contract The post Army aims for speed in new $10B software contract first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jared Serbu, Federal News Network

item.title: Over 600M cyberattacks target Windows users every day
item.description: Windows operating system users face more than 600 million cyberattacks every day, according to Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson. The attacks are on the rise.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: An electric aircraft the military has its eyes on can take off with only 150 feet of runway
item.description: Natural disasters like Hurricane Helene in North Carolina illustrate the need for aircraft capable of accessing remote or disaster-stricken areas with minimal infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Phone bans in class wanted, but not all-day restrictions: Poll
item.description: 68% of U.S. adults said in a Pew Research Center survey conducted Sept. 30-Oct. 6, 2024 say they approve of such rules.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: October is Cybersecurity Awareness Month. Here's how to stay safe from scams
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — October is Cybersecurity Awareness Month, which means it's the perfect time to learn how to protect yourself from scams. “Scams have become so sophisticated now.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA MORGA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: US to probe Tesla's 'Full Self-Driving' system after pedestrian killed in low visibility conditions
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — The U.S. government's road safety agency is investigating Tesla's “Full Self-Driving” system after getting reports of crashes in low-visibility conditions, including one that killed a pedestrian.
item.dc:creator: TOM KRISHER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: This gigayacht has a glass-bottom pool and stunning underwater lounge
item.description: An architecture firm has unveiled the concept for a 454-foot gigayacht that combines luxury, innovation and style and can accommodate 22 guests and 52 crew members.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Alabama man arrested in SEC social media account hack that led the price of bitcoin to spike
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An Alabama man was arrested Thursday for his alleged role in the January hack of a U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission social media account that led the price of bitcoin to spike, the Justice Department said. Eric Council Jr.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Hit the lights: Mets outfit Citi Field for electric light shows
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Mets are lighting up Citi Field, even before their batters step up to the plate. About 70 front-office staffers scattered around the ballpark for over five hours to install 3,500 strips of LED lights on each railing ahead of New York's first home postseason game.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Instagram rolls out new features to prevent sextortion scams targeting teens
item.description: Sextortion occurs when a victim is coerced to provide sexually explicit photos or videos of themselves and threatened with them being shared publicly.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation
